title: Entity 31
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Entity 31: "Maws"}}__NOTOC__ {{Content Warning|bad_content=Gore|page= {{Entity Class|no=31|Hab=Many|Agg=2|Int=2|Dan=4}}{{Blurimage|file=Maw.jpg|caption=A witness's drawing of a Maw.|hovertext=Goremorph}} ==Description== '''Maws'''Scientifically known as "Vorax", meaning "voracious" in Latin. are highly ravenous entities born from the prolonged hunger of wanderers. If a living wanderer is in a state of extreme starvation, there is a small chance that they will transform into a '''Maw'''. The process in which this happens is exceptionally gruesome. As the transformation begins, the victim's lips will rapidly pull and fold back into the surrounding flesh, with the rest of their body soon following. Everything under the skin also folds around once the change gets past the jaws, turning the victim inside out in mere seconds. This process does not leave the body in the shape it was before, instead rendering it a limbless mass of innards trailing along in a serpentine form. The alteration fuses the organs together so the new '''Maw''' does not fall apart. '''Maws''' are able to sense the location of nearby meat; the closer the entity is, the more accurately it can hone in on the food. While this most strongly applies to food of good quality, it also functions with partially spoiled foods to a lesser degree. Nonetheless, when a '''Maw''' eats, most of the mass from the meal is converted into its own mass, with rare documented instances of the entity growing well over three times their original size. While it is unknown what the size limit to their growth is — if any — no '''Maws''' have ever been known to grow larger than the approximate size of a bus. This is due to the increased difficulty for larger '''Maws''' of finding enough food to sustain themselves. Wanderers sometimes leave poisoned food around for '''Maws''' in what are fittingly known as "Maw Traps", including safe food along with it to entice the entity closer. Since '''Maws''' will be less inclined to eat the poisonous meal on its own but do not actually recognize its danger, they will usually fall for the bait. To make sure other wanderers do not fall for the same trap, some of the deadly food will be arranged in a triangle around the rest of it; failing that, the triangle will simply be drawn with something like chalk. Maw Traps have raised ethical concerns considering how wanderers unfamiliar with Backrooms culture might fall victim to them, but many wanderers make them regardless, as '''Maws''' are far more likely to find them than uneducated wanderers. ==Behavior== The hunger of a '''Maw''' is unceasing; they will consume humans and any entity that predominately consists of flesh, including their own kind. Entities that are only made of a small ratio of flesh are still sometimes liable to be attacked. '''Maws''' also often eat the bones of their prey once their teeth have grown predatory enough to do so. Their sheer voracity leaves them vulnerable to engaging in fights they cannot win. [[Entity 5-A|Amalgamations]] that absorb them typically use them as tentacles to help move around or ensnare prey, as '''Maws''' will immediately attempt to devour the organic parts of Entity 5-A and thus get their mouths fused to the entity. Moreover, as a result of their extremely voracious nature, they do not hesitate to attack much larger entities and typically ignore any wounds inflicted on them by their targets. Throwing sizeable chunks of fresh meat in their direction may rarely distract them, as they lack the thinking skills to deliberately single out moving prey of equal quality, likely especially due to their sheer hunger. This strategy cannot be relied upon, however. The strength of the entity's detection of different types of nearby foods is directly correlated with how much '''Maws''' will prioritize those foods over others. Despite this, '''Maws''' often eat other types of edibles, such as vegetables and even fungi. Nonetheless, if there is plenty of better food nearby, spoiled and non-meat meals are sometimes ignored. ==Biology== '''Maws''' do not have conventional biology; rather, they are made of the remains of the humans they once were. When a '''Maw''' is first formed, all the formerly-internal organs are merged together enough to function as one body. As that very body is turned inside out and flesh is twisted beyond recognition, many bones are broken and shattered, with some occasionally ending up protruding out of the entity in some instances. Despite all this, none of it appears to be painfulIt is unknown if the same can be said for their past human selves in their final moments., as '''Maws''' show no signs of even so much as being stunned after initially forming. Indeed, it is rumored that they do not feel any pain at all, though evidence suggests this is untrue and that they simply tolerate it exceedingly well out of sheer, blind hunger. Nonetheless, they are known to possess other senses, namely touch, hearing, and some form of sight or echolocation, despite not having any of the natural receptors usually necessary for any of those. Unlike the rest of a '''Maw''', the teeth are left exactly the same after the transformation. This makes eating the raw meat of prey somewhat inconvenient in the short term, but the longer the entity lives, the sharper the teeth become until they are capable of easily cutting through flesh like those of any carnivore. However, it often does not even take this long; when the '''Maw''' gains mass, the teeth alter much faster at a distinctly visible rate. Feeding also has another noticeable effect besides growth; as the human remains that make up their bodies grow, they merge together more. Given enough growth, this eventually leads to the entity having a much smoother texture and thus being able to move faster. The way in which '''Maws''' move is somewhat anomalous. They move by undulating their bodies like snakes, though eyewitnesses have described the movements as clumsier and less graceful. These movements do not entirely justify their speed, not only due to their imperfect slithering but also their rough shape. Their speed differs with size, starting with ones that have yet to eat their first meal moving at an approximate average of 20mphOr 32kmph., making them faster than most adult men. Even under the circumstances of being chased, the odds of one being able to outrun a '''Maw''' of that caliber — let alone a larger one — are generally unfavorable. Their hunger is commonly believed to drive them faster than any human's fear ever could for them. While '''Maws''' cannot utilize whatever anomaly allows them to move so fast to fly, they are still able to swim just as easily as they move on land, moving through the water much like eels. As such, if being pursued in a body of water, one should always attempt to find land so one has a better chance of escape. ==Discovery== '''Maws''' have seemingly existed for all of human history in the Backrooms. Carvings from the long-extinct [[Entity 70|Homo Callidus]] depict what appear to be members of their race turning into '''Maws''', heavily indicating that the fleshy entities predate even homo sapiens. Millennia later but still centuries ago, '''Maws''' were widely reputed to be wanderers who had been cursed by a Backrooms-dwelling wendigo, a myth that still exists among a very small number of wanderers to this day. {{Footnotes}} ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial= '''Open Author & Credits Info''' |final= '''Close Author & Credits Info''' |body= '''Author:''' [[User:Forknife468152|Forknife]] '''Image:''' Drawn by me, image cropped with [https://pixlr.com/editor/ Pixlr]
